ocr.text: Mapping the Protein Fold Universe with CamTube
Table 2. Parameters for Cβ-Cα-N-C' and Cβ-Cα-C'-N dihedral angles used in the CamTube force field that encode the propensity of the amino
acids for different regions in the Ramachandran map.
Amino acid Angle deﬁnition Function type Vn γ n

Asn, Asp, His, Pro Cβ Cα C N 9 10.0 0 1
Ala, Gln, Ile, Phe, Thr, Tyr, Val Cβ Cα C N 9 0.0 0 1
Fig 9. Ramachandran maps of non-Gly residues after the introduction of the dihedral potentials in the CamTube force field. Residues are grouped
according to their propensity for particular regions in the Ramachandran map: a) Arg, Cys, Met, Leu, Ser, Trp; b) Asn, Asp, His; c) Ile, Phe, Thr, Tyr, Val; d)
Ala, Gln; e) Glu, Lys; f) Pro.
